title: Markets, Tokens, and Grain Flow
content:
# Economics And Material Culture

## Trade, exchange, and pressure
Trade pressure is clearest when Rouen bread prices rose during the winter shortage. A softer economic read still keeps Bakers in Rouen used household bread tokens and Rouen bakers were paid in bread scrip. Stage scenes in Rouen, mainly around 1421-12-01 and 1422-01-01. Keep uncertainty bounded: treat Bakers in Rouen used household bread tokens and Rouen bakers were paid in bread scrip as the working read rather than hard fact. Sources: Curated archive note on bakers' scrip (curated input); Petition of the Rouen bakers (petition 3); Municipal price ledger of Rouen (folio 12r).

## Sceneable material cues
Material culture stays concrete through the fact that Rouen market debt was tracked with wax tally tablets. Keep the material cue sceneable enough to name outright: Rouen market debt was tracked with wax tally tablets. Sources: Abbey barley accounts (leaf 9v).

## Writer's working line
Use the economy as daily pressure rather than abstract background. Treat prices, tools, and exchange routines as something characters handle in motion. Stage scenes in Rouen, mainly around 1421-12-01 and 1422-01-01. Keep uncertainty bounded: treat Bakers in Rouen used household bread tokens and Rouen bakers were paid in bread scrip as the working read rather than hard fact. Sources: Curated archive note on bakers' scrip (curated input); Petition of the Rouen bakers (petition 3); Municipal price ledger of Rouen (folio 12r).

## Coverage Notes
Coverage currently supports economics and daily life.

title: What the City Said vs. What the Ledgers Say
content:
# What the City Said vs. What the Ledgers Say

The working version for the novel keeps the grain-bell timing dispute visible as public confusion, not as a solved footnote. The latest generated synthesis stays below as the evidence-backed baseline.

- Treat the prime-versus-terce contradiction as a queue-management problem the characters can feel.
- Keep merchant-hoarding talk in dialogue and rumor, never in omniscient narration.
- Use the blue-lantern shrine story for tone, not confirmation.
generated_markdown:
# Rumors And Contested Accounts

## Rouen grain bell
On Rouen grain bell, accounts diverge over Rouen grain bell rang after prime. The stable contrast points toward Rouen grain bell rang after terce. Keep the stronger line explicit as contrast only: Rouen grain bell rang after terce. Sources: Council grain ordinance (article 7); Council addendum on the grain bell (addendum 2).

## Saint Romain shrine lanterns
On Saint Romain shrine lanterns, accounts diverge over Saint Romain shrine lanterns were said to burn blue before the thaw in Rouen. Keep the uncertainty explicit rather than folding it into settled description. Sources: Dockside ballad of Saint Romain (verse 4).

## Circulation
These accounts circulate through different witnesses. They cluster around Rouen grain bell rang after prime. Repeated details include Saint Romain shrine lanterns were said to burn blue before the thaw in Rouen. Sources: Council grain ordinance (article 7); Dockside ballad of Saint Romain (verse 4).

## Operational effect
When scenes touch this dispute, treat Rouen grain bell rang after prime as contested rather than silently settled. If a counter-line is needed on the page, use Rouen grain bell rang after terce. Sources: Council grain ordinance (article 7); Council addendum on the grain bell (addendum 2).

## Coverage Notes
Coverage currently supports ritual and rumor.

title: House Style Decisions
content:
# Author Decisions

## Rouen docks
Drafting default: depict Rouen docks as narrow, freezing, and lantern-lit. Rationale: Author decision to unify harbor scenes even when evidence is thin.. Nearby canon context to keep in view: Rouen bread prices rose during the winter shortage and Bakers in Rouen used household bread tokens. Keep this in the author-decisions lane rather than presenting it as settled canon in strict-fact outputs.

## Coverage Notes
Coverage is usable for drafting and stays bounded by approved canon.
